task.name: Add GET /api/installments route
task.action: Create `src/app/api/installments/route.ts`. Export `dynamic = 'force-dynamic'` and a `GET` handler. Require a Better Auth session (401 otherwise). Parse `status` query param — default `"ACTIVE"`, allowed `"ACTIVE"` or `"ALL"`, otherwise 400. Build a Zod schema or manual check for the param. Query `db.expenseTransaction.findMany` filtering by `userId: session.user.id`, `isInstallment: true`, and `installmentStatus: status === 'ALL' ? { in: ['ACTIVE', 'COMPLETED'] } : 'ACTIVE'`. Include `account`, `expenseType`, `expenseSubcategory`. Order `installmentStartDate: 'desc'`. Return `NextResponse.json({ installments }, { status: 200 })`. Handle 500 in a try/catch with `console.error`.
task.verify: Run `npx vitest run src/app/api/installments/route.test.ts` after task 10 adds tests. For now, `npm run lint` and `npm run build` must pass, and the file must compile with the existing tsconfig.

task.name: Add PATCH /api/installments/[id] route
task.action:
Create `src/app/api/installments/[id]/route.ts`. Export `dynamic = 'force-dynamic'` and a `PATCH` handler.
Define `ServerPatchInstallmentSchema` as a Zod object with only these optional keys: `name` (string 1..100), `amount` (string positive number), `installmentStartDate` (string), `expenseTypeId` (string), `expenseSubcategoryId` (string nullable). Use `.strict()` so unknown keys are rejected. If the parse fails, return 400 with flattened details.
Also explicitly reject body keys `installmentDuration`, `accountId`, `tagIds`, `isInstallment`, `remainingInstallments`, `monthlyAmount`, `lastProcessedDate`, `installmentStatus`, `parentInstallmentId`, `isSystemGenerated` with `400 { error: "Field not editable: " }` before the Zod parse (since `.strict()` will catch them too, but we want a cleaner error).
Auth check (401). Fetch `existing = db.expenseTransaction.findUnique({ where: { id, userId: session.user.id } })`. 404 if missing. If `!existing.isInstallment` → 400 `{ error: "Not an installment" }`. If `existing.installmentStatus === "CANCELLED"` → 404.
If `expenseSubcategoryId` is provided and non-null, verify it belongs to the user and to the target expense type (mirror the logic in `src/app/api/expense-transactions/[id]/route.ts` PATCH).
Build `updateData: Record = {}`. If `name !== undefined` → set. If `expenseTypeId !== undefined` → set. If `expenseSubcategoryId !== undefined` → set (null allowed). If `amount !== undefined`: `const newAmount = parseFloat(amount); updateData.amount = newAmount; updateData.monthlyAmount = newAmount / existing.installmentDuration!;`.
If `installmentStartDate !== undefined`: - `const oldStart = existing.installmentStartDate!; const newStart = new Date(installmentStartDate);` - Normalize both to `setHours(0, 0, 0, 0)`. - `updateData.installmentStartDate = newStart; updateData.date = newStart;` - If `newStart.getTime() > oldStart.getTime()`: - `const children = await db.expenseTransaction.findMany({ where: { parentInstallmentId: id, userId: session.user.id } });` - `const childrenToDelete = children.filter(c => c.date < newStart);` - Build `operations` array. For each child: push `db.financialAccount.update({ where: { id: child.accountId, userId: session.user.id }, data: { currentBalance: { increment: parseFloat(child.amount.toString()) } } })` then push `db.expenseTransaction.delete({ where: { id: child.id } })`. - `updateData.remainingInstallments = existing.installmentDuration;` - `updateData.lastProcessedDate = null;` - Else (newStart ≤ oldStart): no child operations.
Push `db.expenseTransaction.update({ where: { id, userId: session.user.id }, data: updateData, include: { account: true, expenseType: true, expenseSubcategory: true } })` as the last operation.
`const results = await db.$transaction(operations);` — the parent update is the last element; extract via `results[results.length - 1]`.
After the transaction: `await onExpenseTransactionChange(existing.accountId, existing.date);` and if the start date changed, also `await onExpenseTransactionChange(existing.accountId, new Date(installmentStartDate));`.
Return the updated row with 200.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. File compiles. Full test suite is deferred to task 10.

task.name: Add DELETE /api/installments/[id] route
task.action:
In the same `src/app/api/installments/[id]/route.ts`, add a `DELETE` handler.
Auth check (401). Fetch `existing` same as PATCH. 404 if missing. If `!existing.isInstallment` → 400 `{ error: "Not an installment" }`. If `existing.installmentStatus === "CANCELLED"` → 404.
Fetch `const children = await db.expenseTransaction.findMany({ where: { parentInstallmentId: id, userId: session.user.id } });`.
Build `operations: PrismaPromise[] = []`. For each child, push `db.financialAccount.update({ where: { id: child.accountId, userId: session.user.id }, data: { currentBalance: { increment: parseFloat(child.amount.toString()) } } })`.
Push `db.expenseTransaction.deleteMany({ where: { parentInstallmentId: id, userId: session.user.id } })`. Push `db.expenseTransaction.delete({ where: { id, userId: session.user.id } })`.
`await db.$transaction(operations);` `await onExpenseTransactionChange(existing.accountId, existing.date);`
Return `200 { message: "Installment deleted successfully", deleted: true }`.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`.

task.name: Reject parent-installment delete on legacy route
task.verify: Run `npm run lint`. The existing test `'does not hard-delete installment expense — marks as CANCELLED instead'` will now fail — this is expected and will be fixed in Task 10. Do NOT run the full test suite yet.

task.name: Add useInstallmentsQuery hook
task.action:
Create `src/hooks/useInstallmentsQuery.ts`. Follow the structure of `src/hooks/useExpenseTransactionsQuery.ts` but simpler (no optimistic updates).
Export: - `Installment` type mirroring the API response (reuse most fields from `ExpenseTransaction` type). - `useInstallmentsQuery(options?: { status?: 'ACTIVE' | 'ALL' })`: - `useQuery` with key `['installments', { status: options?.status ?? 'ACTIVE' }]` and fetch `/api/installments?status=ACTIVE|ALL`. - `useMutation` for update: fetch `PATCH /api/installments/[id]`. On success invalidate `['installments']`, `['expenseTransactions']`, `RECENT_TRANSACTION_QUERY_KEYS.recentTransactions`, `['accounts']`, `['cards']`, `['netWorth']`, `['netWorthHistory']`, `['monthlySummary']`, `['budgetStatus']`, `['annualSummary']`, `['expenseBreakdown']`. - `useMutation` for delete: fetch `DELETE /api/installments/[id]`. On error show toast `toast.error("Failed to delete installment", { description: errorMessage, duration: 6000 })`. On settled invalidate the same set above. - Return `{ installments, isLoading, error, updateInstallment, deleteInstallment, deleteInstallmentAsync, isUpdating, isDeleting }`. - `useInstallmentQuery(id: string, opts?: { enabled?: boolean })`: - `useQuery` with key `['installments', id]` and fetch `GET /api/installments/{id}` — but we do NOT have a GET-by-id endpoint. Instead, derive from the list: look up in cached list first, and if not found, fall back to `fetch('/api/installments')` (simpler) OR add a new `GET /api/installments/[id]` route.
**Decision:** Since the edit drawer needs full data quickly, add a lightweight `GET /api/installments/[id]` handler inside `src/app/api/installments/[id]/route.ts` that fetches the single row (auth + `isInstallment: true` + `installmentStatus !== "CANCELLED"`, else 404). Then `useInstallmentQuery` calls it directly. This keeps the hook consistent with `useExpenseTransactionQuery`.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. File compiles.

task.name: Add EditInstallmentDrawer component
task.action:
Create `src/lib/validations/installments.ts` with the `updateInstallmentSchema` defined in spec §4.3.
Create `src/components/installments/EditInstallmentDrawer.tsx`. Mirror the structure of `src/components/forms/EditExpenseDrawer.tsx`: - Props: `{ open, onOpenChange, installmentId }`. - Use `useInstallmentQuery(installmentId, { enabled: open })` and `useInstallmentsQuery()` (for `updateInstallment` + `deleteInstallment` + `isUpdating` + `isDeleting`). - React Hook Form with `zodResolver(updateInstallmentSchema)`, `mode: "onTouched"`. - While `isFetching` → render `SkeletonEditExpenseDrawerForm`. - On error → same error state as EditExpenseDrawer. - Fields (see spec §4.3): - `name` → `Input`. - `amount` → `Input type="number"`, with a live-computed `FormDescription` under it showing `Monthly: ₱{watchedAmount / duration}` formatted. - **Account** → plain `
` with `{installmentData.account.name}` plus a label. Non-editable. - **Duration** → plain `
` with `{installmentData.installmentDuration} months`. Non-editable. - `installmentStartDate` → Popover + Calendar (same as existing drawer's installmentStartDate field, no `disabled` on Calendar). - `expenseTypeId` → `Select`. - `expenseSubcategoryId` → `Select` conditional on `selectedExpenseType.subcategories?.length`. - Footer: `Update installment` button, `Cancel` DrawerClose, `Separator`, `Delete installment` destructive outline button. - `DeleteDialog`: title `"Delete installment"`, description `"This will permanently delete this installment and all {paidCount} payment records. This cannot be undone."` where `paidCount = installmentData.installmentDuration! - installmentData.remainingInstallments!`. `onConfirm`: call `deleteInstallment(installmentId)`, close drawer, show success toast. - `onSubmit`: build payload `{ id, name, amount, installmentStartDate: formatDateForAPI(values.installmentStartDate), expenseTypeId, expenseSubcategoryId: values.expenseSubcategoryId === "none" ? null : values.expenseSubcategoryId }`. Call `updateInstallment(payload)`.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Component compiles.

task.name: Add InstallmentTable (desktop)
task.action:
Create `src/components/installments/InstallmentTable.tsx`.
Props: `{ installments, onEdit, onDelete }` per spec §4.4.
Use `Table`, `TableHeader`, `TableBody`, `TableRow`, `TableHead`, `TableCell` from `@/components/ui/table`. Plain `.map()` — no TanStack Table needed.
Columns as specified in spec §4.4. Helpers inside the file: - `formatCurrency(v: string | number) => string` using `toLocaleString('en-PH', { minimumFractionDigits: 2, maximumFractionDigits: 2 })`. - `computeNextPayment(row) => Date | null` — per spec rule. - `paid = installmentDuration - remainingInstallments`. - Progress bar: `
`.
Status badge: `ACTIVE` → `Active`, `COMPLETED` → `Completed`.
Actions column: ` onEdit(row.id)}>` and ` onDelete(row.id, row.name)}>`.
Assume non-empty array — empty state handled by parent.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`.

task.name: Add InstallmentCard (mobile)
task.action:
Create `src/components/installments/InstallmentCard.tsx`.
Props: `{ installment, onEdit, onDelete }` per spec §4.5.
Root: `
onEdit(installment.id)}>`.
Inside: - Row 1: icon (`Icons.addExpense size={20} text-primary`) + name (bold) + status badge on the right. - Row 2: `
{expenseType.name}{subcategory ? ` > ${subcategory.name}` : ""}`. - Info grid (`grid grid-cols-[auto_1fr] gap-x-3 gap-y-1 text-sm`): - "Monthly" / `₱{formattedMonthly}` - "Duration" / `{duration} months` - "Progress" / `{paid} of {duration}` + progress bar (same markup as table). - "Next payment" / `{formattedNextPayment}` or `"—"` if COMPLETED. - Actions row at bottom: `
` containing edit and delete icon buttons. Both buttons' `onClick` must call `e.stopPropagation()` before invoking the callback.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`.

task.name: Wire Tabs + Installments tab into the Expenses page
task.action:
Modify `src/app/expenses/page.tsx`:
1. Import `Tabs, TabsList, TabsTrigger, TabsContent` from `@/components/ui/tabs`. 2. Introduce a new component `InstallmentsTabContent` at the bottom of the file (or in a new file `src/components/installments/InstallmentsTabContent.tsx` — prefer the file). It contains: - `showCompleted` state (boolean, default false). - `selectedInstallmentId` state. - `editDrawerOpen` state. - `deleteDialogOpen` + `installmentToDelete` state. - `const { installments, isLoading, error, deleteInstallment, isDeleting } = useInstallmentsQuery({ status: showCompleted ? 'ALL' : 'ACTIVE' });` - A `Switch` with label "Show completed" controlling `showCompleted`. - Mobile section (`md:hidden`): skeleton list / empty state / `InstallmentCard.map()`. - Desktop section (`hidden md:block`): skeleton table / empty state / `InstallmentTable`. - Error block matches Expenses page. - `EditInstallmentDrawer` bound to `selectedInstallmentId` + `editDrawerOpen`. - `DeleteDialog` bound to the installment-to-delete state; confirm calls `deleteInstallment(installmentToDelete.id)` with description `"This will permanently delete this installment and all {paidCount} payment records. This cannot be undone."`. 3. In `Expenses`, wrap the existing body (everything under `` except the `CreateExpenseTransaction*`/`EditExpenseDrawer` mounts) in: ```tsx
Transactions Installments
{/* existing content */}
``` 4. Keep `CreateExpenseTransactionSheet`, `CreateExpenseTransactionDrawer`, and `EditExpenseDrawer` mounted above the Tabs (unchanged). 5. The top-level error state (when `error` from `useExpenseTransactionsQuery`) and the initial "no expenses yet" EmptyState must remain scoped only to the Transactions tab — do not hide the Installments tab when the user has no regular expenses.
**Empty-state refactor:** The current page shows the "No expenses yet" empty state at the top level before rendering anything else. Move that check inside the `` block so the Installments tab still renders even when the user has no regular transactions.
task.verify: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Run `npm run dev` locally and manually verify both tabs render, switching works, and the existing Transactions tab behavior is unchanged. Confirm the Installments tab shows active installments and the toggle reveals completed ones.

task.name: Update existing tests for rejected parent-installment delete and add new test coverage
task.action:
**Modify** `src/app/api/expense-transactions/[id]/route.test.ts`: - Delete the test `'does not hard-delete installment expense — marks as CANCELLED instead'`. - Add a new test `'rejects parent installment delete with 400'` that: - Mocks `db.expenseTransaction.findUnique` to return `{ ...mockExistingExpense, isInstallment: true }`. - Calls `DELETE` and asserts `response.status === 400`, `data.error` matches `/Use DELETE \/api\/installments/`, and `db.$transaction` was not called.
**Create** the test files listed in spec §8.1–§8.3 (use the `generate-tests` skill via the `qa-pipeline` agent as part of the post-execution checklist — this task covers setting up the test expectations and running the suite to green).
After all tests are authored, run: - `npx vitest run` — full suite must pass. - `npm run lint`. - `npm run build`.
Then create `docs/installment-management-verification.md` documenting the test results and manual verification steps.
task.verify: `npx vitest run` returns all green. `npm run lint` and `npm run build` both exit 0. A verification document is created at `docs/installment-management-verification.md` summarizing results.
